title: Revisiting deep learning models for tabular data
methodology: Implemented MLP, ResNet, FT-Transformer, and others under a unified hyperparameter-tuning protocol (Optuna, identical budgets). Evaluated on 11 tabular benchmarks including California Housing, Adult, Higgs, Year, Microsoft.
contributions: A simple MLP with carefully chosen hyperparameters often matches more elaborate tabular DL architectures.
FT-Transformer (feature-tokeniser + standard transformer encoder) is the strongest single tabular DL method tested.
Across 11 datasets, no single architecture dominates — including GBDTs, which the paper treats as a tough baseline they often fail to beat.
weaknesses: The hyperparameter tuning is exhaustive; production-grade tuning budgets may not reach the same gains.
Does not cover modern self-supervised pre-training approaches.
emerald_relevance: Strongest credible single tabular DL architecture for inclusion in section 5.8. The paper's own caveats about GBDT competitiveness inform section 4.3's sober positioning.
search_query: Gorishniy revisiting deep learning tabular FT-Transformer NeurIPS 2021
notes: Introduced FT-Transformer (feature-tokeniser + transformer). Strongest single tabular DL in independent benchmarks.
